source: Elevated RDW can indicate increased red blood cell production variability, which may be seen in early stages of Type 2 Diabetes.
Anemia (low Hgb) is a common complication of Type 2 Diabetes due to factors like impaired erythropoiesis and increased red blood cell destruction.
Anemia (low Hct) is a common complication of Type 2 Diabetes due to factors like impaired erythropoiesis and increased red blood cell destruction.
Elevated WBC count can indicate infection or inflammation, which may be associated with Type 2 Diabetes.
Thrombocytosis (high platelet count) can be associated with Type 2 Diabetes, potentially due to increased platelet activation.
Microcytosis (low MCV) can be seen in iron deficiency anemia, which is a common complication of Type 2 Diabetes.
Low MCH indicates smaller red blood cells, which can be seen in iron deficiency anemia, a common complication of Type 2 Diabetes.
Low MCHC indicates decreased hemoglobin concentration in red blood cells, which can be seen in iron deficiency anemia, a common complication of Type 2 Diabetes.
Anemia (low RBC count) is a common complication of Type 2 Diabetes due to factors like impaired erythropoiesis and increased red blood cell destruction.
